name: Instagram Profile Picture
description: Ensures the correct account is active, downloads an image from a URL, then sets it as the Instagram profile picture.

params.username: The Instagram username that should be active
params.imageUrl: Direct URL to the image file to use as profile picture

steps.goal: Open Instagram. Dismiss any popups or dialogs. Check if the account '{{username}}' is currently logged in and active. Look at the profile tab or the username at the top of the screen. If '{{username}}' is the active account, you are done. If a different account is active, long-press the profile picture icon in the bottom-right corner to open the account switcher. If '{{username}}' appears in the list, tap it to switch. If '{{username}}' is not in the list, mark as failed.

correct_account_active.description: The Instagram account '{{username}}' is currently logged in and active (visible as the current user)

steps.goal: Download this image and save it to the device gallery in an album called {{albumName}}: {{imageUrl}}

Open Instagram. If you see any popup or dialog (like 'Not now', 'Skip', account suggestions, or a Google sign-in prompt), dismiss it by tapping 'Not now', 'Skip', 'X', or pressing Back. Navigate to your own profile by tapping the profile icon in the bottom-right corner of the screen. Once on your profile page (you should see 'Edit profile' button), tap 'Edit profile'.

You are on the Edit Profile screen. Complete ALL of these sub-tasks in order:

1) Tap 'Edit picture or avatar' near the top of the screen. A bottom sheet menu will slide up.

2) In the bottom sheet, tap 'Choose from library'. The correct result is Instagram's photo picker showing a circular crop preview and a grid of photo thumbnails.

3) Once in the photo picker, tap the 'Recents' dropdown label above the photo grid (NOT the Android system Recents button at the bottom of the screen).

4) The album selection opens. Find and tap the '{{albumName}}' album. If you don't see it, look for 'See all' next to Albums and tap that first, then find '{{albumName}}'.

5) The album shows the downloaded image. Tap it to select it.

6) You should now see a circular crop preview. Tap 'Done' or the checkmark button (top-right) to confirm and save the new profile picture.

7) After tapping Done, Instagram may show 'Processing...' briefly. Once you return to the Edit Profile screen, the profile picture has been updated successfully.

Verify the profile picture was changed. Navigate to your profile page (tap the profile icon in the bottom-right or press Back until you see the profile page). Check that the profile picture area shows a new image (not the default avatar). The profile picture should look different from the default silhouette. If it appears updated, mark done.
